channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B

item.title: Nonreciprocal spin-wave excitations in Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Aastha Jain and Dheeraj Kumar Singh

We explore the nonreciprocity of spin-wave excitations in the Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnet on a square lattice. Our study reveals that the propagation of spin-wave excitations exhibit nonreciprocal behavior, i.e., spin waves traveling in opposite directions display asymmetry in energy dispersion $ω(\m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054411] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aastha Jain and Dheeraj Kumar Singh

We explore the nonreciprocity of spin-wave excitations in the Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnet on a square lattice. Our study reveals that the propagation of spin-wave excitations exhibit nonreciprocal behavior, i.e., spin waves traveling in opposite directions display asymmetry in energy dispersion ω(q)≠…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054411] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonreciprocal spin-wave excitations in Rashba-Hubbard ferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Aastha Jain and Dheeraj Kumar Singh
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Large anisotropic magnetoresistance in $α$-MnTe induced by strain
item.description:
Author(s): Bao-Feng Chen, Jie-Xiang Yu, and Gen Yin

$α\text{-MnTe}$ is a $p$-type semiconducting altermagnet with a Néel temperature near $300\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\text{K}$. Due to the altermagnetic nature, $\mathcal{PT}$ symmetry is broken and Kramers degeneracy is lifted in the valence band maxima along the $\mathrm{Γ}\text{−}K$ line and the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054412] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bao-Feng Chen, Jie-Xiang Yu, and Gen Yin

α-MnTe is a p-type semiconducting altermagnet with a Néel temperature near 300K. Due to the altermagnetic nature, PT symmetry is broken and Kramers degeneracy is lifted in the valence band maxima along the Γ−K line and the A point. However, the energy difference is found to be small, and any small s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054412] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Large anisotropic magnetoresistance in $α$-MnTe induced by strain
item.dc:creator: Bao-Feng Chen, Jie-Xiang Yu, and Gen Yin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Tunneling spectroscopy of two-dimensional superconductors with the quantum twisting microscope
item.description:
Author(s): Nemin Wei, Felix von Oppen, and Leonid I. Glazman

The authors develop here a theory for momentum-resolved tunneling spectroscopy of van der Waals superconductors using the quantum twisting microscope and demonstrate how it can directly map the anisotropic superconducting gap in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064502] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nemin Wei, Felix von Oppen, and Leonid I. Glazman

The authors develop here a theory for momentum-resolved tunneling spectroscopy of van der Waals superconductors using the quantum twisting microscope and demonstrate how it can directly map the anisotropic superconducting gap in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064502] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunneling spectroscopy of two-dimensional superconductors with the quantum twisting microscope
item.dc:creator: Nemin Wei, Felix von Oppen, and Leonid I. Glazman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Quantum phase transition driven by competing intralayer and interlayer hopping in bilayer nickelates
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Zhu, Wei Qin, Ping Cui, and Zhenyu Zhang

Bilayer nickelates exhibit high-temperature superconductivity under proper hydrostatic pressure or epitaxial strain, signifying the emergence of quantum phase transitions whose physical mechanisms remain unclear. Using a minimal bilayer Hubbard model incorporating only the Ni ${d}_{3{z}^{2}−{r}^{2}}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075116] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Zhu, Wei Qin, Ping Cui, and Zhenyu Zhang

Bilayer nickelates exhibit high-temperature superconductivity under proper hydrostatic pressure or epitaxial strain, signifying the emergence of quantum phase transitions whose physical mechanisms remain unclear. Using a minimal bilayer Hubbard model incorporating only the Ni d3z2−r2 orbitals, we de…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075116] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum phase transition driven by competing intralayer and interlayer hopping in bilayer nickelates
item.dc:creator: Xiaoyu Zhu, Wei Qin, Ping Cui, and Zhenyu Zhang

item.title: High harmonic tracking of ultrafast electron dynamics across the Mott to charge-density-wave phase transition
item.description:
Author(s): Marlena Dziurawiec, Jessica O. de Almeida, Mohit Lal Bera, Marcin Płodzień, Maciej Lewenstein, Tobias Grass, Ravindra W. Chhajlany, Maciej M. Maśka, and Utso Bhattacharya

Different insulator phases compete with each other in strongly correlated materials with simultaneous local and nonlocal interactions. It is known that the homogeneous Mott insulator converts into a charge-density-wave (CDW) phase when the nonlocal interactions are increased, but there is ongoing de…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075117] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Marlena Dziurawiec, Jessica O. de Almeida, Mohit Lal Bera, Marcin Płodzień, Maciej Lewenstein, Tobias Grass, Ravindra W. Chhajlany, Maciej M. Maśka, and Utso Bhattacharya

Different insulator phases compete with each other in strongly correlated materials with simultaneous local and nonlocal interactions. It is known that the homogeneous Mott insulator converts into a charge-density-wave (CDW) phase when the nonlocal interactions are increased, but there is ongoing de…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075117] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: High harmonic tracking of ultrafast electron dynamics across the Mott to charge-density-wave phase transition

item.title: Superconductivity in an imbalanced bilayer Hubbard model: Enhanced $d$-wave and weakened ${s}^{±}$-wave pairing
item.description:
Author(s): Ziying Jia, Xun Liu, and Mi Jiang

We investigate the bilayer model with two layers of imbalanced densities coupled by interlayer hybridization. Using the large-scale dynamical cluster quantum Monte Carlo simulation, we discovered that increased hybridization induces a transition in the superconducting pairing from $d$ wave to ${s}^{…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075119] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ziying Jia, Xun Liu, and Mi Jiang

We investigate the bilayer model with two layers of imbalanced densities coupled by interlayer hybridization. Using the large-scale dynamical cluster quantum Monte Carlo simulation, we discovered that increased hybridization induces a transition in the superconducting pairing from d wave to s± wave …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075119] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity in an imbalanced bilayer Hubbard model: Enhanced $d$-wave and weakened ${s}^{±}$-wave pairing

item.title: Charge-transfer-induced depth redistribution of surface spin states in the topological insulator $\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}\mathrm{T}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Ryu Yukawa, Yuki Higuchi, Yuichiro Toichi, Ryota Itaya, Kyosuke Nomura, Miho Kitamura, Kenichi Ozawa, Hiroshi Kumigashira, and Kazuyuki Sakamoto

The spatial distribution of interfacial spin states is a critical factor governing the efficiency of spin-injection and spin-detection in spintronic devices. In this study, we use angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) to investigate the evolution of the two types of spin-polarized surfac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075412] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryu Yukawa, Yuki Higuchi, Yuichiro Toichi, Ryota Itaya, Kyosuke Nomura, Miho Kitamura, Kenichi Ozawa, Hiroshi Kumigashira, and Kazuyuki Sakamoto

The spatial distribution of interfacial spin states is a critical factor governing the efficiency of spin-injection and spin-detection in spintronic devices. In this study, we use angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) to investigate the evolution of the two types of spin-polarized surfac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075412] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Charge-transfer-induced depth redistribution of surface spin states in the topological insulator $\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}\mathrm{T}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}$

item.title: Casimir interactions between two parallel graphene sheets carrying steady-state drift currents
item.description:
Author(s): Modi Ke, Dai-Nam Le, and Lilia M. Woods

We investigate the fluctuation-induced Casimir interactions between two parallel graphene sheets carrying steady-state drift currents. The graphene properties are modeled based on the shifted Fermi disk model to capture the nonequilibrium optical response of the system. We find that the drift curren…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075414] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Modi Ke, Dai-Nam Le, and Lilia M. Woods

We investigate the fluctuation-induced Casimir interactions between two parallel graphene sheets carrying steady-state drift currents. The graphene properties are modeled based on the shifted Fermi disk model to capture the nonequilibrium optical response of the system. We find that the drift curren…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075414] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026

item.title: Quantum critical point followed by Kondo-like behavior due to Cu substitution in the itinerant antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{La}}_{2}{({\mathrm{Cu}}_{x}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{1−x})}_{7}$
item.description:
Author(s): Atreyee Das, Siham Mohamed, Raquel A. Ribeiro, Tyler J. Slade, Juan Schmidt, Chandan Setty, Sergey L. Bud'ko, and Paul C. Canfield

${\text{La}}_{2}{\text{Ni}}_{7}$ is an itinerant magnetic system with a small ordered moment of $∼ 0.1{µ}_{B}/\text{Ni}$ and a series of antiferromagnetic (AFM) transitions at ${T}_{1}=61.0\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}, {T}_{2}=56.5\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$, and ${T}_{3}=42.2\…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085114] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Atreyee Das, Siham Mohamed, Raquel A. Ribeiro, Tyler J. Slade, Juan Schmidt, Chandan Setty, Sergey L. Bud'ko, and Paul C. Canfield

La2Ni7 is an itinerant magnetic system with a small ordered moment of ∼ 0.1µB/Ni and a series of antiferromagnetic (AFM) transitions at T1=61.0K, T2=56.5K, and T3=42.2K. M(H), and ρ(H) isotherms as well as constant field M(T) and ρ(T) measurements on single-crystalline samples manifest a complex, an…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085114] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum critical point followed by Kondo-like behavior due to Cu substitution in the itinerant antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{La}}_{2}{({\mathrm{Cu}}_{x}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{1−x})}_{7}$
item.dc:creator: Atreyee Das, Siham Mohamed, Raquel A. Ribeiro, Tyler J. Slade, Juan Schmidt, Chandan Setty, Sergey L. Bud'ko, and Paul C. Canfield

item.title: Quantum Hall to chiral spin liquid transition in a triangular lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Cesar A. Gallegos, Rafael M. Magaldi, Andrew Millis, and Steven R. White

We investigate the weak interaction integer quantum Hall (IQH) phase, the intermediate interaction phase identified as a chiral spin liquid (CSL) and the transition between them in the triangular lattice Hofstadter–Hubbard model at a density of one electron per site in an orbital magnetic field corr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064412] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cesar A. Gallegos, Rafael M. Magaldi, Andrew Millis, and Steven R. White

We investigate the weak interaction integer quantum Hall (IQH) phase, the intermediate interaction phase identified as a chiral spin liquid (CSL) and the transition between them in the triangular lattice Hofstadter–Hubbard model at a density of one electron per site in an orbital magnetic field corr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064412] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Hall to chiral spin liquid transition in a triangular lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Cesar A. Gallegos, Rafael M. Magaldi, Andrew Millis, and Steven R. White
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Field theory of Borromean supercounterfluids
item.description:
Author(s): Anatoly Kuklov, Leo Radzihovsky, and Boris Svistunov

A Borromean counter-flow superfluid (BCSF) is a highly entangled state of N>2 atomic components. It occurs in the Mott insulating phase where the total flow is arrested. The authors solve here the problem of the dynamic field theoretical description of BCSF. The new model features Borromean hydrodynamics as a low-energy theory representing the symmetry of the U(1)N factor group. It reveals a counter-flow AC Josephson effect, and generically predicts a first-order phase transition into a BCSF state in dimensions greater than two.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060504] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anatoly Kuklov, Leo Radzihovsky, and Boris Svistunov

A Borromean counter-flow superfluid (BCSF) is a highly entangled state of N>2 atomic components. It occurs in the Mott insulating phase where the total flow is arrested. The authors solve here the problem of the dynamic field theoretical description of BCSF. The new model features Borromean hydrodynamics as a low-energy theory representing the symmetry of the U(1)N factor group. It reveals a counter-flow AC Josephson effect, and generically predicts a first-order phase transition into a BCSF state in dimensions greater than two.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060504] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Field theory of Borromean supercounterfluids
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kuklov, Leo Radzihovsky, and Boris Svistunov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Coexisting massive and massless Dirac fermions in moiré-reconstructed bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Mohit Kumar Jat, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Aveek Bid

Here, the authors show that a moiré superlattice in bilayer graphene aligned with hexagonal boron nitride drives a topological reconstruction of its electronic bands. Magnetotransport reveals the emergence of massless Dirac fermions in moiré minibands, coexisting with the parent bilayer’s massive carriers. The work highlights moiré engineering as a simple and versatile route to tunable band topology.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L081102] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohit Kumar Jat, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Aveek Bid

Here, the authors show that a moiré superlattice in bilayer graphene aligned with hexagonal boron nitride drives a topological reconstruction of its electronic bands. Magnetotransport reveals the emergence of massless Dirac fermions in moiré minibands, coexisting with the parent bilayer’s massive carriers. The work highlights moiré engineering as a simple and versatile route to tunable band topology.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L081102] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Coexisting massive and massless Dirac fermions in moiré-reconstructed bilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Mohit Kumar Jat, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Aveek Bid
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Selective Kondo screening and strange metallicity in sliding Dirac semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Hanting Zhong, Shuxiang Yang, Chao Cao, Xiao-Yong Feng, and Jianhui Dai

Kondo screening (KS) of local moments in normal metals typically leads to hybridized conduction and valence bands separated by a Kondo gap, resulting in an insulating state at half-band filling. We show a dramatic change of this scenario in a Dirac-semimetal-based correlated system—a bilayer honeyco…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075111] Published Thu Feb 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hanting Zhong, Shuxiang Yang, Chao Cao, Xiao-Yong Feng, and Jianhui Dai

Kondo screening (KS) of local moments in normal metals typically leads to hybridized conduction and valence bands separated by a Kondo gap, resulting in an insulating state at half-band filling. We show a dramatic change of this scenario in a Dirac-semimetal-based correlated system—a bilayer honeyco…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075111] Published Thu Feb 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Selective Kondo screening and strange metallicity in sliding Dirac semimetals
item.dc:creator: Hanting Zhong, Shuxiang Yang, Chao Cao, Xiao-Yong Feng, and Jianhui Dai

item.title: Electronic band structure and anisotropic dielectric response of ${\mathrm{PtS}}_{2}$ from angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, spectroscopic ellipsometry, and density functional theory: An indirect-gap hyperbolic van der Waals semiconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Debasis Dutta, Marcin Rosmus, Grazia Giuseppina Politano, Chia-Nung Kuo, Carlo Rizza, Gianluca D'Olimpio, Chin Shan Lue, Amit Agarwal, and Antonio Politano

By combining angle-resolved photoemission, density functional theory, and variable-angle spectroscopic ellipsometry, we determine the bulk electronic structure and anisotropic optical response of platinum disulfide ($\mathrm{Pt}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$). Momentum-resolved maps collected at 60 and 85 eV rev…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085407] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Debasis Dutta, Marcin Rosmus, Grazia Giuseppina Politano, Chia-Nung Kuo, Carlo Rizza, Gianluca D'Olimpio, Chin Shan Lue, Amit Agarwal, and Antonio Politano

By combining angle-resolved photoemission, density functional theory, and variable-angle spectroscopic ellipsometry, we determine the bulk electronic structure and anisotropic optical response of platinum disulfide (PtS2). Momentum-resolved maps collected at 60 and 85 eV reveal a single holelike bra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085407] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic band structure and anisotropic dielectric response of ${\mathrm{PtS}}_{2}$ from angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, spectroscopic ellipsometry, and density functional theory: An indirect-gap hyperbolic van der Waals semiconductor

item.title: Origin of moiré potentials in ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterobilayers: Contributions from lattice reconstruction and interlayer charge transfer
item.description:
Author(s): Youwen Wang, Nanya Gao, and Qingjun Tong

Moiré superlattices formed in ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterobilayers have emerged as an exciting platform to explore the quantum many-body physics. The key mechanism is the introduction of moiré potentials for the band-edge carriers induced by the lateral modulation of interlayer inte…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085408] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Youwen Wang, Nanya Gao, and Qingjun Tong

Moiré superlattices formed in WS2/WSe2 heterobilayers have emerged as an exciting platform to explore the quantum many-body physics. The key mechanism is the introduction of moiré potentials for the band-edge carriers induced by the lateral modulation of interlayer interactions. This trapping potent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085408] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Origin of moiré potentials in ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterobilayers: Contributions from lattice reconstruction and interlayer charge transfer
item.dc:creator: Youwen Wang, Nanya Gao, and Qingjun Tong
